name: Ebony Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Ebony. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Low power for organic material preservation

energyDensity.description: Low energy to preserve wood fibers

scanSpeed.description: Fast scanning to prevent heat damage

passCount.description: Single gentle pass for delicate surfaces

power.description: Typical range for delicate organic materials like wood to avoid thermal damage; starting at 100W for controlled cleaning of light contaminants on Ebony. Max safe ~500W to prevent charring (based on general Nd:YAG laser cleaning studies for woods, e.g., Journal of Cultural Heritage, 2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds balance cleaning efficiency and heat input; slower for heavy contamination (500-1000 mm/s), faster for light (2000-3000 mm/s). Recommended 1500 mm/s for uniform Ebony surface prep (industry standards from IPG Photonics manuals and laser cleaning process optimization).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for minimal heat-affected zone on low-conductivity woods like Ebony; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond reduces thermal damage but increases cost. 20 ns typical for 1064 nm cleaning without substrate ablation (laser physics research, e.g., Optics & Lasers in Engineering, 2020).

repetition_rate.description: 20-50 kHz balances speed and heat accumulation for organic materials; higher rates (>50 kHz) risk overheating Ebony due to poor thermal conductivity. Recommended 30 kHz for efficient contaminant removal (academic literature, e.g., Applied Surface Science, 2019).

energy_density.description: Fluence below Ebony's ablation threshold (~5 J/cm² at 1064 nm for dark woods); 0.5-2 J/cm² for light cleaning, up to 4 for heavy. Recommended 1.5 J/cm² safe for contamination removal without fiber damage (ablation threshold studies, e.g., Journal of Applied Physics, 2017; conservative for Ebony's low reflectivity).

spot_size.description: Smaller spots (100-200 μm) for fine detail on Ebony grain, larger (300-500 μm) for broad areas. Recommended 200 μm for precise industrial cleaning (equipment specs from Coherent and Trumpf laser systems).

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contaminants, multi-pass (2-3) for heavy or layered removal on woods. Recommended 2 passes for complete Ebony cleaning without overexposure (industry case studies, e.g., laser restoration of wooden artifacts in Heritage Science, 2021).

overlap_ratio.description: 50-70% ensures uniform coverage and avoids gaps/striping on irregular surfaces like Ebony; lower for speed, higher for thoroughness. Recommended 60% for optimal process (process optimization studies, e.g., Lasers in Manufacturing and Materials Processing, 2022).
